root_motivation: The LLM orchestrating MQO operations needs a faithful, context-safe, and stable picture of every dataset it touches so it can decide the correct next operation (filter, aggregate, top_n, etc.) without ever receiving the raw rows. The summary IS the protocol boundary: without a bounded, informative summary the dataset-handle abstraction is fictional and the HARD_ROW_CAP=1000 leak in mqo-mcp-server is the only thing standing between the model and context overflow.
user_persona: Rust library consumer (mqo-mcp-server, dh-ops, future fleet members) that calls summarize(&dataset, &cfg) and capabilities(&dataset) to produce the LLM-visible compact view of any Dataset returned by dh-store.

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --release 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | grep -oP '[0-9]+ passed' | grep -oP '[0-9]+'

acceptance_criteria.description: summarize over a 10_000-row fixture produces a summary whose sample.len() <= sample_cap AND whose serialized JSON size <= max_bytes.
Numeric stats (min/max/sum/mean/distinct) match a hand-computed golden for a 5-value Int fixture.
Categorical top-k is correct and capped at topk; date span min/max are correct.
capabilities omits Pivot for a single-dimension dataset and includes Pivot for a >=2-dimension dataset.
At least one deterministic null note is generated for a nullable fixture; a dominant-group note is generated when one group >= 50%; notes are stable (same input -> same output).
When a dataset would exceed max_bytes, the sample is truncated (not the stats) and a truncation note is added; stats for all columns are still present.
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings is clean; round-trip serialization to/from JSON is lossless.

scope: summarize(&Dataset, cfg: &SummaryCfg) -> DatasetSummary with bounded sample and stats
Per-column ColStats: numerics (min/max/sum/mean/distinct), categoricals (distinct + top-k with counts), dates (min/max/span via top_k)
Deterministic notes: null counts, dominant group >= 50%, date spans
capabilities(&Dataset) -> Vec<Capability> with Pivot rule (>= 2 dims)
Hard max_bytes self-check: truncates sample (never stats); panics only if zero-sample still exceeds budget
Pure/deterministic library — no I/O, no async, no randomness
non_goals: Not a row-dump or pass-through — summarize must never return more rows than sample_cap
Not an async or streaming API — pure synchronous fn
Not responsible for storing or evicting dataset handles — that is dh-store's job
Not computing expensive statistical measures (median, percentiles, histograms) — only cheap O(n) stats
Not providing a CLI binary — library crate only (--target lib)
Not computing ground-truth numerical correctness for the benchmark scorer — separate concern

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the LLM need a summary instead of raw rows?
five_whys_trace.a: Raw rows can exceed the context window. The HARD_ROW_CAP=1000 in mqo-mcp-server is an ad-hoc ceiling; the model also can't decide its next op without shape/stats.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the bound on the summary matter specifically?
five_whys_trace.a: If summary size is unbounded, the bounded-handle contract is fictional — rows still flood the context even if called 'a summary'.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just extend the existing HARD_ROW_CAP truncation at the MQO engine layer?
five_whys_trace.a: Truncation gives the model a partial view with no stats; the model still can't decide whether to filter, aggregate, or pivot next without knowing shape, nulls, or dominant groups.

five_whys_trace.q: Why must the output be deterministic?
five_whys_trace.a: Non-deterministic summaries would produce flapping orchestrator decisions; the model would see different data on retries and potentially choose conflicting operations.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is this a separate crate rather than inlining into mqo-mcp-server?
five_whys_trace.a: Every dh-* fleet member (dh-ops, dh-pivot, future ops) needs to emit a summary after every operation. A shared crate enforces the single canonical summary shape and the max_bytes contract across the fleet.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should 'distinct' count nulls as a distinct value?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes — nulls are treated as one additional distinct value when null_count > 0, consistent with SQL semantics.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What does 'date span correct' mean for the test?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Date min/max are surfaced via top_k entries with label fields ('min'/'max') since ColStats has no dedicated span field.
